evidenceTypesSupplied: Broad continental autosomal reference-panel similarity summary from the local fixture
World Ancestry sample PDF rows preserved as sample-only output-format examples
Regional percentage model and panel metadata explicitly unavailable for the local fixture
sectionsUnavailable: No calibrated regional ancestry percentage model is supplied for the local fixture.
No local exact homeland, community, migration path, family matching, relative matching, or segment-coordinate data is supplied.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A broad East Asian continental reference-panel similarity summary is available, but no calibrated regional percentage model is supplied for this local fixture.
resultRows.description: The local fixture supplies derived broad autosomal context only; it does not supply exact percentages or segment calls.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This is broad world-ancestry context only. It is not a precise regional percentage, homeland, nationality, community label, or identity statement.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A calibrated regional percentage model and reference-panel metadata are unavailable in this fixture and are not inferred.
resultRows.description: Missing model evidence is stated explicitly instead of filling in unsupported ancestry, genealogy, or identity details.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Missing regional model evidence means this run cannot provide precise percentages, homeland, community, migration, segment-ancestry, or relative-matching information.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample cover identifies a World Ancestry Report for Jennifer Doe, Version Vdr 1.0, from Galatea Bio, followed by a welcome narrative from the Galatea team.
resultRows.description: Observed on page 1 of the local sample PDF; this row documents report identity and opening structure only.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed on page 1 of the local sample PDF; this row documents report identity and opening structure only. This is preserved as observed sample-report structure only, not as a local ancestry estimate or identity claim.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample hierarchy shows 100% total, 95.9% European, 4.1% American, and 2.9% East Asian, accompanied by a map, donut chart, and region legend.
resultRows.description: The sample overall ethnicities section demonstrates a nested hierarchy and global map presentation. These percentages are sample-only output structure, not local ancestry findings.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The sample overall ethnicities section demonstrates a nested hierarchy and global map presentation. These percentages are sample-only output structure, not local ancestry findings. This is preserved as observed sample-report structure only, not as a local ancestry estimate or identity claim.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample hierarchy lists Scandinavian 36.6%, Iberian 28.2%, Eastern Europe 16.4%, Northwest European 8.8%, and Basque 7.0% under European ancestry.
resultRows.description: Observed European subregion rows in the sample PDF. Do not normalize these values or use them as local ancestry estimates.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed European subregion rows in the sample PDF. Do not normalize these values or use them as local ancestry estimates. This is preserved as observed sample-report structure only, not as a local ancestry estimate or identity claim.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample hierarchy lists East Asian 2.9% and Arctic 2.9%; the Arctic ethnicity card is labeled with region EAST ASIA.
resultRows.description: Observed on page 1 and page 10. The page 10 text layer contains overlapping title text, so the extracted row uses the page 1 Arctic value plus the rendered card body and region.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Observed on page 1 and page 10. The page 10 text layer contains overlapping title text, so the extracted row uses the page 1 Arctic value plus the rendered card body and region. This is preserved as observed sample-report structure only, not as a local ancestry estimate or identity claim.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample report includes generic narrative pages for America, Europe, Levantine and Middle East, Africa, East Asia, South Asia, Southeast Asia, and Oceania.
resultRows.description: These pages document background report sections only; they do not provide user-specific local ancestry calls.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: These pages document background report sections only; they do not provide user-specific local ancestry calls. This is preserved as observed sample-report structure only, not as a local ancestry estimate or identity claim.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample chromosome painting page shows chr1-22 bars with a legend for EUR, AMR, AFR, SAS, OCE, EAS, SEA, and LME.
resultRows.description: The page explains that ancestry labels are assigned to portions of chromosomes and rendered as color-coded segments. The sample does not expose segment coordinates.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The page explains that ancestry labels are assigned to portions of chromosomes and rendered as color-coded segments. The sample does not expose segment coordinates. This is preserved as observed sample-report structure only, not as a local ancestry estimate or identity claim.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample states that Galatea was not able to ascertain ancestry calls in transparent or white chromosome-painting regions.
resultRows.description: This caveat is part of the observed output format and should be retained whenever sample chromosome painting is shown.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: This caveat is part of the observed output format and should be retained whenever sample chromosome painting is shown. This is preserved as observed sample-report structure only, not as a local ancestry estimate or identity claim.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample states that the report only shows ethnicity cards applicable to the customer.

resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The card pages demonstrate a percent bar, region label, map, cultural narrative, and empty or blank Scientific details heading. This is preserved as observed sample-report structure only, not as a local ancestry estimate or identity claim.

ancestryScopeSummary.autosomal: Autosomal world ancestry can summarize broad reference-panel similarity when a model is supplied; this fixture does not supply calibrated local percentages.
ancestryScopeSummary.referencePanel: Reference panels provide comparison context, not a fixed identity label or exact origin.
ancestryScopeSummary.sampleReport: The World Ancestry sample PDF demonstrates output structure, including hierarchy rows, map legend, chromosome painting, ethnicity cards, and methodology text, but those sample values are not local findings.

whatThisDoesNotMean: This is not an exact global or regional ethnicity percentage for the local fixture.
This does not identify a precise homeland, tribe, community, nationality, migration route, or living relatives.
This is not a family-matching or genealogy-relative report.
This is not a segment-level local ancestry call for the local fixture.
This is not a health or disease-risk report.

references.title: MedlinePlus Genetics: pros and cons of direct-to-consumer genetic testing

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample methodology describes assigning ancestry labels to DNA segments from whole-genome or SNP-array data with machine-learning classification and error correction.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Use as observed sample methodology only; the local fixture does not include a calibrated G-Nomix model, reference panel, or segment calls.

genotypeSummary.genotype: sample total 100%; European 95.9%; American 4.1%; East Asian 2.9%
genotypeSummary.effect: The sample demonstrates a top-level ancestry hierarchy with map and legend presentation.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Use as sample output structure only; do not reuse these percentages as local ancestry, identity, homeland, or community findings.

genotypeSummary.genotype: Scandinavian 36.6%; Iberian 28.2%; Eastern Europe 16.4%; Northwest European 8.8%; Basque 7.0%
genotypeSummary.effect: The sample demonstrates regional rows and ethnicity-card pages beneath a continental ancestry grouping.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Use as sample output structure only; do not infer local regional percentages, precise homeland, migration path, or relatives.

genotypeSummary.genotype: colored sample segments plus transparent or white no-call regions
genotypeSummary.effect: The sample demonstrates chromosome-painting visualization and an explicit no-call caveat without segment-coordinate data.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Use as observed output structure only; do not create local segment ancestry calls without calibrated model evidence.

genotypeSummary.genotype: broad East Asian reference-panel similarity; no percentage calibration supplied
genotypeSummary.effect: The local fixture supplies broad autosomal context without calibrated ethnicity percentages.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Report as broad ancestry context only; do not convert it into exact percentages, homeland, identity, relatives, or health interpretation.

genotypeSummary.genotype: calibrated regional percentage model and reference-panel metadata unavailable
genotypeSummary.effect: The local fixture intentionally omits calibrated regional ancestry percentages and segment-level model provenance.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Mark regional ancestry, exact homeland, chromosome-painting calls, relatives, family matching, migration path, and community affiliation unavailable instead of inferring them.

missingInputs: No calibrated ancestry model, reference-panel metadata, or regional-percentage output is supplied for the local fixture.
No family-matching, living-relative, migration-route, exact-homeland, or community-affiliation data is supplied.
No segment-coordinate table or local chromosome-painting calls are supplied for the local fixture.
uncertainty: Broad reference-panel similarity should not be treated as a precise ancestry percentage or identity.
Sample PDF percentages are useful for output-format replication only and must remain separate from local fixture evidence.
The fixture is a validation artifact, not a complete world-ancestry inference pipeline.
limitations: Raw genome data is not included in this validation result.
The report does not emit raw SNP tables, VCF, FASTQ, BAM, CRAM, or relative-matching data.
World Ancestry sample PDF rows are preserved as sample-only examples and do not validate local percentage estimates.
